title: FLEX(-) Bacterial DNA Extraction Protocol for Nanopore and Hybrid Whole Genomic Sequencing
author: Jack Boulé

steps.title: Label a 1:1 matched set of...
steps.instruction: Label a 1:1 matched set of PowerBead tubes and LoBind tubes for each isolate accession (use the same sample ID format across all tubes). Then label the collection vessels using the same IDs—either: 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes (recommended for ≤48 samples), or a 0.8 mL 96-well PCR plate (recommended for >48 samples). - Note: Each option requires a different magnet type (96-well plate magnet if using a PCR plate, and a standing tube magnet for Eppendorfs (such as the Thermofisher DynaMag)); ensure you have the appropriate magnet.

steps.title: Pre-equilibrate a temperature block or water...
steps.instruction: Pre-equilibrate a temperature block or water bath to 56 °C (either may be used based on personal preference/equipment availability).

steps.title: Attach the Qiagen Vortex Adapter top...
steps.instruction: Attach the Qiagen Vortex Adapter top to a Vortex-Genie (or equivalent vortex mixer). Note: The adapter top holds up to 24 samples ; use multiple adapter tops in parallel if processing more than 24 samples at once.

steps.title: Calculate how much of each enzyme...
steps.instruction: Calculate how much of each enzyme is required for given batch size (See Table 1).

steps.title: Calculate the amount of each reagent...
steps.instruction: Calculate the amount of each reagent required for a given batch size (See Table 2).

steps.title: Remove the enzyme (Proteinase K) from...
steps.instruction: Remove the enzyme (Proteinase K) from the freezer and allow it to thaw. Keep at 4 °C until ready for use.

steps.title: Aliquot 300 µL of TE Buffer into each 0
steps.instruction: Aliquot 300 µL of TE Buffer into each 0.5mm Glass Powerbead Tube.

steps.title: Inoculate each PowerBead tube with isolated...
steps.instruction: Inoculate each PowerBead tube with isolated bacterial colonies using either a heaping 1 µL loop or half of a 10 µL loop , while minimizing agar carryover . Note: After inoculation, gently spin/rotate the loop in the tube to help disperse and homogenize the biomass before proceeding.

steps.title: Pipette 8μL Sarkosyl (10% sol), (Using...
steps.instruction: Pipette 8μL Sarkosyl (10% sol), (Using a P10) into each Powerbead tube, directly into the solution

steps.title: Slowly and gently pipette mix up and down 2-3 times
steps.instruction: Slowly and gently pipette mix up and down 2-3 times. Over-pipetting may lead to foam that makes later steps far more difficult. In the case this occurs, allow foam to settle, gently tapping the tube to speed up the process. Some foam is expected.

steps.title: Insert Powerbead tubes into a Qiagen...
steps.instruction: Insert Powerbead tubes into a Qiagen Vortex Adapter Top, with the cap side facing inward.

steps.title: Place the top onto a Vortex...
steps.instruction: Place the top onto a Vortex Genie or an equivalent vortex.

steps.title: Set the vortex speed to 9...
steps.instruction: Set the vortex speed to 9 (or the max speed on an equivalent Vortex), and allow the top to spin for 5 minutes. Note: If using an alternative Vortex, samples may spin faster than expected, causing over fragmentation of DNA, this can be corrected for by lowering the speed until fragment analysis returns optimal results)

steps.title: Turn off the vortex and remove the adapter top
steps.instruction: Turn off the vortex and remove the adapter top.

steps.title: Place the tubes back onto a...
steps.instruction: Place the tubes back onto a rack, maintaining the original format.

steps.title: Pipette 5 µL of Proteinase K...
steps.instruction: Pipette 5 µL of Proteinase K (20 mg/ml) (Using a P10) into each Powerbead tube, directly into solution.

steps.title: Slowly and gently pipette mix up and down 2-3 times
steps.instruction: Slowly and gently pipette mix up and down 2-3 times.

steps.title: Place Powerbead tube into a 56...
steps.instruction: Place Powerbead tube into a 56 °C dry incubator or a 56 °C water bath, maintaining the original layout of the tubes on their respective racks.

steps.title: Incubate for 15 min
steps.instruction: Incubate for 15 min.

steps.title: Remove from incubator
steps.instruction: Remove from incubator.

steps.title: Transfer 225 µL of the solution...
steps.instruction: Transfer 225 µL of the solution from Powerbead tubes into either a 0.8 ml, 96 well MIDI/PCR Plate, or a 1.5 ml Eppendorf tube (see note in step 1) Note: You may find that the powerbeads are sucked up into the pipette. This can be solved by tilting the tube sideways and drawing solution from the side beads are not sitting on.

steps.title: Add 225 µL of SPRI Select Beads into each well/tube
steps.instruction: Add 225 µL of SPRI Select Beads into each well/tube. Note: a 1:1 ratio of SPRI select beads to solution ensures you select for fragments of all sizes. In the case you changed any of the initial volumes used, adjust SPRI bead volume accordingly.

steps.title: Mix by pipetting up and down 3-5 times
steps.instruction: Mix by pipetting up and down 3-5 times. - Homogeneity is critical for this step as too little mixing will prevent complete DNA binding.

steps.title: Place onto appropriate magnet (96 well...
steps.instruction: Place onto appropriate magnet (96 well Illumina Magnet for MIDI plates, Thermo DYNAMag Magnet for Eppendorfs).

steps.title: Leave on magnet until lysate appears...
steps.instruction: Leave on magnet until lysate appears clear (3-5 min), and a pellet has formed on the well/tube side wall. Note: If using eppendorfs, rotating the tube left and right while on the magnet can speed up the process of pellet formation.

steps.title: With the plate/tube STILL on the...
steps.instruction: With the plate/tube STILL on the magnet, carefully remove the supernatant with a P1000 set to 1000 µL. Avoid touching the pellet. If any pellet is sucked into the pipette, put it back into the well/tube, towards the magnet side, and leave for another 2 minutes before removal of supernatant. It is highly recommended that you review the mechanism behind SPRI select beads to best understand and proactively troubleshoot proper lysate removal.

steps.title: Remove the tube/plate from the magnet
steps.instruction: Remove the tube/plate from the magnet.

steps.title: Pipette 600 µL of 80% ethanol...
steps.instruction: Pipette 600 µL of 80% ethanol into each well/tube directly onto the pellet.

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in the ethanol...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in the ethanol by pipetting up and down until homogeneous. Note: Some pellets may require scraping with the tip of the pipette to help them better homogenize.

steps.title: Place tube/plate back onto the magnet
steps.instruction: Place tube/plate back onto the magnet.

steps.title: Allow pellet to reform (2-3 min)
steps.instruction: Allow pellet to reform (2-3 min).

steps.title: Remove supernatant with a P1000 set to 1000 µL
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant with a P1000 set to 1000 µL.

steps.title: Remove tube/plate from magnet again
steps.instruction: Remove tube/plate from magnet again.

steps.title: Pipette 600 µL of 80% ethanol...
steps.instruction: Pipette 600 µL of 80% ethanol into each well/tube directly onto the pellet.

steps.title: This time, do not resuspend the...
steps.instruction: This time, do not resuspend the pellet fully by mixing, simply allow the ethanol to sit on the pellet.

steps.title: Place the tube/plate back onto the magnet
steps.instruction: Place the tube/plate back onto the magnet.

steps.title: Wait 2-3 min
steps.instruction: Wait 2-3 min.

steps.title: Remove and discard the supernatant with...
steps.instruction: Remove and discard the supernatant with a P1000 set to 1000 µL, carefully avoiding the pellet.

steps.title: Allow pellet to air dry for...
steps.instruction: Allow pellet to air dry for 10 min , monitoring and wicking away any residual pooling; do not over-dry to the point of visible cracking. That said, it is better to over-dry than under-dry, as any remaining ethanol will act as a contaminant in your final eluate.

steps.title: Once dry, take the tube/plate off the magnet
steps.instruction: Once dry, take the tube/plate off the magnet.

steps.title: Add 100 µL of Molecular Grade...
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL of Molecular Grade Water (ddH2O) directly onto the pellet. Note: if you are finding that your yield is too low using 100µL, you may decrease the volume accordingly.

steps.title: Resuspend pellet fully , by mixing...
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellet fully , by mixing up and down with a pipette.

steps.title: Place tube/plate back onto the magnet
steps.instruction: Place tube/plate back onto the magnet.

steps.title: Wait 2-3 min for pellet to reform
steps.instruction: Wait 2-3 min for pellet to reform.

steps.title: Transfer all 100 µL of the...
steps.instruction: Transfer all 100 µL of the supernatant into a labelled Lo-Bind Eppendorf tube, this is your final DNA solution, which can now be stored at -80 °C for up to 10 years.

steps.title: You may now discard the tubes/plate...
steps.instruction: You may now discard the tubes/plate with the pellet (DNA is now in the supernatant you just realiquoted)

steps.title: Quantify DNA with a Qubit Fluorometer,...
steps.instruction: Quantify DNA with a Qubit Fluorometer, using an eluate input of 4 µL, and a High Sensitivity Reagent input of 196 µL. (See Oxford Nanopore Technologies NO-MISS Protocol for acceptable eluate concentrations)

steps.title: Check for contamination using a Nanodrop or equivalent device
steps.instruction: Check for contamination using a Nanodrop or equivalent device. (See Oxford Nanopore Technologies NO-MISS Protocol for acceptable 260/280 and 260/230 ratios)

steps.title: Optionally
steps.instruction: Optionally: Check for high fragment length retention using a Bioanalyzer or Fragment Analyzer (See Oxford Nanopore Technologies NO-MISS Protocol for acceptable fragment lengths)

steps.title: Table 1
steps.instruction: Table 1: %s %s

steps.title: Table 2
steps.instruction: Table 2: %s %s

steps.title: With the following steps, you must...
steps.instruction: With the following steps, you must put on and take off the plate/tube from the magnet as stated. Failure to do so will result in the complete loss of eluate.

description: See Troubleshooting Guide for related fixes
